LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APRIL-29-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES WAS BASED ON THE CONCEALMENT CLAIM, WHICH PLAINTIFF DISMISSED. THEREFORE, THERE IS NO LONGER A BASIS FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. RICHARD MILLER, Esq. specially appearing for Quill and Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar as moot. The demurrer targets the fourth cause of action for concealment and plaintiff dismissed that claim on April 15, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. RICHARD MILLER, Esq. specially appearing for Quill and Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Grant without leave to amend. The claim for punitive damages was based on the concealment claim, which plaintiff dismissed. Therefore, there is no longer a basis for exemplary damages.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
